title: PR1SMLITE
description: PR1SMLITE is a Minecraft shader built for budget and integrated GPUs. If you want the most performance this is the one.
body:
![A luscious cave exposing expansive lighting. This image is including moss, glow berries, and lava as the main showcase.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/bc16c077a721d6aef3025dda31fa1941fd4e0f46.jpeg)

#

PR1SMLITE is a Minecraft shader built from dozens of iterations for budget and integrated GPUs. This shaderpack includes sharpening, bloom, color clarity, and a shadow rendering process. If you want the most performance out of shaders, but don't want bad quality, this is your next choice.
Developed on an AMD Ryzen 5 7520U (with Radeon 610M integrated-GPU), it was made to achieve high framerates at low costs. When using Iris and Sodium we achieved larger framerates above most shaders due to the less complex rendering processes. Using no special upscaling tricks, no heavy rendering algorithms, but only using a very little amount of math that makes staying close to vanilla performance possible.
Outsourcing more frames or performance has become the new want of today, and this shader fills in that slot in the shader-side of Minecraft. If your GPU struggles with every other shaderpack, or if you want a level of beauty while still wanting a playable game, this shader was built exactly for you. Download it and try it for yourself, no configurations needed.

## Installation:

This installation process requires both [Sodium](https://modrinth.com/mod/sodium) and [Iris](https://modrinth.com/mod/iris) for installation. Once you have installed Sodium and Iris, you can place them into your mods folder for Minecraft! After this make sure you have at least ran Minecraft one time to make sure your shaderpack folder is built. Now since the game has already ran one time, open to where your Minecraft Java folder is and search for a "shaderpack" folder. Open this folder and drop PR1SMLITE inside. You can now open Minecraft again and you should see the shaderpack if you open your options menu, go to Video settings, and look for a button that says "Shaders" or "Shaderpacks." Now you should be able to find "PR1SMLITE" as a option, once selected you can enable shaders and experience the true looks of lightweight shaders
